Explain why electromagnetic waves do not require a medium for propagation?
questions.answer: Electromagnetic waves do not require a medium for propagation because they are transverse waves and can exist in free space.

questions.q: A plane electromagnetic wave has its electric field vector along the y-axis and propagation vector along the x-axis. Sketch the electric field vector and the propagation vector.
The intensity of an electromagnetic wave is proportional to the square of the amplitude of the wave.
The electromagnetic wave is a transverse wave.
questions.answer: Its electric field vector is perpendicular to the direction of propagation.

questions.q: The electromagnetic waves have the ability to penetrate through materials.
questions.answer: This is due to the ability of electromagnetic waves to penetrate through materials, a property known as the skin depth.

The electromagnetic waves are used in various applications such as
questions.answer: Electromagnetic waves are used in various applications such as Radio, Microwave, Infrared, Visible, Ultraviolet, X-rays, and Gamma rays.
